chapter_tutorial: ^How far did I fall? And where’s Alice? I hope she isn’t hurt…? Alice? Alice!! Alice, answer me, where are you!?
^Oh, thank goodness, I thought I’d lost you. Come here so I can see you, please?
^Press the space bar to switch perspectives…
^I’ll be the judge of that, thank you.
^Oh my god, you don’t need to check me over for injuries, I’m fine! Who are you, Mom?
^I’ll be just as grey as her within a year if you keep running off like you just did, that’s for sure.
^Care to tell me where we are? And why?
^I saw a rabbit! Con, it had a vest on! And– and a pocketwatch! And so I followed it, of course. Now we’re at the bottom of a rabbit hole, I think. Isn’t it amazing?
^A rabbit. A rabbit. You jumped down a rabbit hole, chasing a vest-wearing rabbit.
^…Okay. Sure. Alright. That makes no sense. I tripped on the rabbit hole chasing you across a field, cracked my skull open on a rock, and now I’m dead. Right?
^No, silly! We’re just in a huge, otherworldly lobby at the bottom of an unfathomably deep rabbit hole. Look, they even have refreshments, how nice!
^Ooh, mystery tea, my favourite! It’s even asking me to drink it!
^Already enamoured with the world of Wonderland, Alice reaches enthusiastically for the bottle.
^Ohh, no you don’t. Alice, if you drink “mystery tea” again, I am disowning you. Put it down.
^Okay but the last mystery tea incident was just riverwater, I was only sick for like a day. This is real mystery tea! The genuine article!
^I said put it down. You don’t know what’s in that, it could be poison for all you know.
^Alice gulps down the ‘Drink Me’ labelled bottle, looking entirely too self-satisfied.

c-0: ^Alice, no! Are you stupid!? Spit it out, now!
^Am I st–? Constance, you are such a jerk, I’m fine! Ugh, let go of me!
^…I didn’t mean it like that, I was just–
^A tension weighs in the air… The sisters’ relationship is damaged.
c-1: ^Alice– Alice, look at me. Do you feel well? Do you feel sick? Are you okay?
^Am I okay–? I’m– I’m good, Constance, don’t worry. See? Totally fine!
^I don’t feel sick at all! I promise, okay?
^I won’t do it again, alright? Don’t look so scared.
^A sense of relief fills the air. The sisters’ relationship improves.

after_choice: ^Alright, enough of that! Let’s go explore!
^Don’t think I don’t know what you’re doing.
tutorial_puzzle_chapter: ^Oh come on, let’s just focus on all the cool things in here! Stop worrying so much and take a look around!
^Hm… a door…? It looks like our only way forward, and thereby the only way out of this strange place…
^Well that solves that! First order of business, let’s find ourselves a key!
^Try looking around the room! There are puzzles to be found here in Wonderland… Maybe they’ll offer a way out.
^Keep an eye on both sisters’ perspectives. Wonderland shows itself in different ways to different people.
tutorial_puzzle_complete: ^Woah, look at that! Come on, Con, let’s go, let’s go!
^Skipping out through the door, Alice drags her sister along to their next destination…
MeetCat: ^The girls wander outside the room; a vast and lush forest stretches on for what seems like forever.
^Realizing there’s nowhere else to go, the girls tread carefully down the pathway. They walk until their feet feel sore and everything starts to ache.
^I cannot walk a single step further, my feet hurt and my head’s starting to spin.
^Constance notices her sister looks too tired to continue, but she wisely chooses not to voice this aloud in hopes of keeping Alice calm.
^I suppose we can take a quick break.
^Alice smiles gratefully and happily parks herself down on the path. Silence fills the area around them.
^It’s unsettling and Constance wonders why she can’t hear the wildlife here despite it being midday.
^Maybe it’s cause of the heat? Maybe all the creatures went into hibernation?
^Constance looks at her sister in confusion.
^The animals, you said that it was weird you couldn’t hear the wildlife right?
^Alice I didn’t say that out loud???
^yeahhh it sounds pretty nice though, so don’t worry about it.
^Alice spots something in the distance, just beyond the tree.
^A handsome grin materialized out of thin air.
^Okay hang on, let’s think this through before we jump head first-
^Alice takes off running after the mysterious thing.

c-0: ^Oh my gosh Alice! No!!! Not again!
^Exhausted, but amused by her sister's lively attitude, Constance shelves her worry to chase after Alice quickly.
c-1: ^ALICE I SWEAR, IF YOU LEAD US DOWN ANOTHER RABBIT HOLE, I WILL NOT BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE THINGS I SAY.
^Oh my gosh Con you worry way too much! It’s fine!
^…Why can’t I have one day, just one normal day to myself?
^Frustrated by her sister's carelessness, Constance quickly gives chase to her wayward sibling.

after_choice_2: ^The game of cat and mouse has begun. The strange smile sitting in the distance materializes once more, a sharp looking feline now attached to its grin.
MeetCatPuzzle: ^A cat—oh my gosh it’s a talking cat!!!
^Who Me? I’m just your average run of the mill neighbor. Nothing new to see. Though if it’s a name you are seeking, the Cheshire Cat is just fine.
^Cheshire Cat? That’s an odd name… but I like it. Nice to meet you!
^Alice’s enthusiasm is quite infectious. It’s hard not to smile around her. The Cat gives her a cheeky grin.
^So now then, who might you be dear?
^Oh my! What’s the rush?I was hoping you could stay and have a quick little chat!
^No need. We have no interest in chatting.
^Speak for yourself! I’ve got plenty of questions-
^Constance tugs her sister down the road they came from, her legs rushing forward, her ears trying to drown out Alice’s pleas to stop and talk to The Cat.
^No you don’t, you and I are leaving.
^So soon? We haven’t even sat down for tea yet. It’s quite rude don’t you think?
^Perhaps Constance feels uneasy around this new creature. The Cat doesn’t mean to be intimidating.
^So the Cat tags along. This seems to agitate poor little Constance.
^Oh come now, it’s no fun if you ignore me the whole time. I don’t want to talk to your backsides.
^Then perhaps this is a sign you need to find a path of your own! As you can see, we are quite busy.
^Oh yes, of course I’m sure you're very busy. It must be difficult, waking up in a strange world, unsure what’s up and what’s down.
^The Cat watches in delight as this causes both girls to freeze in their path, eyes wide and seemingly spooked by what they just heard.
^Well, I’ve certainly never seen you here before, and I know everybody, so that means you're new.
^That’s awesome! You know everyone here in Wonderland?
^Of course I do. There isn't a soul here that doesn’t know who I am. I'm quite the intriguing individual.
^So it seems I’ve finally peaked your interest! How wonderful.
^So what is this place? Where is everyone? Are there more people like you here? Are-
^The Cat appears thrilled by this latest discovery. Alice seems puzzled by this revelation.
^I’m more than happy to answer your questions my dear. I only ask that you give me something in return for my troubles.
^Something strange seems to cross Constance's face, her hand tightening on her sisters. She looks ready to bolt.
^Really? You think you can make it out of these enchanted woods without a guide? I can’t tell if you’re foolish or brave.
^The Cat lets out a cackle that feels strange and pitchy and the air shifts uncomfortably around them.
^Thank you for your concern, but I’m sure soon we will stumble upon the people you mentioned.
^What a shame… and here I thought you’d be interested in what I wanted to tell you. After all, it's not often you meet someone associated with our Queen.
^Alice looks shocked and excited while Constance holds a look of relief and suspicion.
^Well aren’t you curious. Maybe you should stay and talk to me after all. Who knows what secrets you'll uncover.
^The Cat gives her a grin thinking it's reassuring, Constance appears unsettled.

c-0: ^We’re good. There's no need to trouble yourself.
^What why? He knows about this place, why aren't you asking him about it??
^Alice enough, please stop questioning my decisions and just follow along.
^I'm questioning your decision because it’s dumb. What is wrong with you? You never act like this?
^Constance appears deeply unsettled by The Cat. Though she has no way of conveying this to Alice without the creature knowing.
^Alice appears frustrated and concerned but the look on her sister's face makes her stay silent for once.
^Soooo that's it? No questions, no comments, nothing? Where's the fun in that my dear?
^No, of course not. You came here for a different purpose. Do you know why?
^The Cat's grin makes Constance's blood boil. Gritting her teeth she ends the conversation and tugs her sister forward.
^Very well, if you won't speak to me just follow the pathway. It will lead you to a special someone you might want to listen to.
^Constance looked at him for a moment, clearly wanting to argue before going against it. She grabbed her sister and walked away, leaving a slightly disgruntled cat to his own devices.
c-1: ^Queen? You’re saying a monarch exists here? Wherever we are?
^Of course silly!  What place wouldn’t have a ruler, especially one as well established as ours…
^Do you think they can help us?
^Oh my, so eager. You're never going to get anywhere if you pounce like that. You my dear need to learn to keep your cards closer to your chest.
^He grins at Constance who fails to maintain eye contact. Alice however seems delighted by The Cat's presence and happily engages.
^So this place, it's called Wonderland right? Is there a reason why? Can you tell me?
^Why do you keep calling this place “Wonderland?”
^Because that's exactly where we are. Wonderland. Though I'm a little surprised you knew the name so quickly.
^The Cat stares at Alice with a mix of shock and something else. How interesting?
^You knew? How in the world did you know that?
^Silence fills the area and The Cat appears a little shocked, but Constance looks exhausted. She rolls her eyes and decides to direct her next question to The Cat.
^I've learned not to question it. It's just simpler. Besides, we've gotten off topic. You told me there's a Queen here. Can we see her?
^The Cat quickly shifts, his whole demeanor seems to shift into a calm ease.
^Well color me surprised. I didn’t expect you to be the impatient one, though I suppose that’s on me. I really need to stop judging a book by its cover.
^Constance appears annoyed by the statement, but she doesn’t seem to voice it out loud, instead waiting patiently for The Cat to speak once more.
^Hmmmm, yes there is a Queen. One that could potentially help you get out of the situation you’ve gotten tangled in.
^The girls wait in tense silence as The Cat begins to mull over his words. He's careful and it makes Constance even more uneasy. She thinks she’s made a mistake by stopping to chat.
^Yes well, there's no use dwelling on the matter, it's not like you'll be able to see her.
^What? Why not? Isn't a Queen supposed to listen to people?
^Well no, that's not her only job...
^Correct you are! Our Queen has far better things to do than listen to the likes of you two.
^That being said, I suppose everyone deserves a chance to be heard...
^Very well, I shall give you both a chance. A chance to meet her.
^Don't thank him just yet. What's the catch?
^Oh Constance. You wound me. If I had known you'd thought so little of me I wouldn't have stopped to chat.
^Despite the harsh statement, The Cat's grin never disappears from his face.
^Just tell us what you want and we can be on our way.
^The Cat only grins at this, delighted in the response Constance has given him.
^Oh my, what a temper. Very well… I don't particularly enjoy your attitude, but I'll help you this once.
^Follow me, my dear. I'll take you to a man that can help you sort out all your troubles.
^Seeing no other choice, both girls decide to follow along. Hopefully The Cat leads them somewhere safe.

after_choice_3: ^With his piece said the girls walk off without waiting for him. To Alice's delight and Constance's annoyance he followed along at a sedated pace.
PartyFight: ^The girls continue to walk forward, The Cat narrating where to go. Constance simply walks forward silent while Alice chatters away about everything.
^She talks about the trees, the birds, the strange world they're in. Each sentence twists something inside Constance—something she tries desperately to ignore.
^And then I drank this crazy looking bottle and the whole room just grew. It was like something out of a movie!
^My, that sounds wonderful! I only wish I could have seen it. I'm sure it was just as enchanting as you described it.
^It was sooo cool! I don't think I've ever seen anything like that happen before. Last
^time I drank weird tea I just got sick.
^The mention of the unknown bottle seems to tip Constance over the edge.
^She reaches for her sister's wrist and pulls tightly. When The Cat moves to follow she gives him a glare and tells him to stay put.
^He listens, though he appears annoyed at being told what to do.
^The girls walk some distance away before Constance turns to her sister, worry etched across her face.
^Alice stop talking to him. Please, just stop talking to him.
^He just gives me the creeps and I don't want to be around him anymore.
^Alice looks confused by this. Why is her sister so worried about a little cat? He hasn't done anything wrong.
^It’s strange. Constance’s feels more manic and less like her usual composed self. What's gotten into her sister?
^Are you feeling alright? You're not usually like this.
^Constance looks at her sister in confusion.
^Feeling alright? Nothing about this place feels alright. Everything is wrong!
^Con, what's happening? Why are you acting like this all of a sudden?
^I'm not acting like anything, I'm only taking this seriously. I mean do you seriously see nothing wrong with that cat? With this place?
^We're lost, we need help, and someone here has the answers to help us. I don't understand why you don't want to take it?
^Oh I don't know, maybe it's the fact that we fell through a weird rabbit hole and ended up in a whole other dimension.
^Maybe it's the fact that we're running around in a never ending forest looking for things that don't exist!
^Or maybe it's because we're talking to a CAT who’s giving us directions?
^Alice watches in horror as her sister begins to spiral, words tumbling out of her. She appears distraught, tugging at her hair to ground herself.
^She needs to break her sister out of this weird cycle, quickly.
^Constance, I need you to breathe, just take a deep breath and focus on me.
^Constance looks ready to argue, but she drops it, trying to calm herself.
^They stand like that for a while, both girls breathing and trying to hold themselves together. Eventually Constance's breathing begins to settle.
^Thank you for that. I'm sorry I freaked out on you.
^Ahhh you're fine. No harm done here. I'm just glad you're feeling better now.
^Constance gives her sister a shy smile. It tenses a little when she remembers The Cat, but she quickly shifts her focus to her sister.
^Talk to me. What's going on up there in that head of yours.
^Constance looks uncomfortable, wanting desperately to drop the subject. Instead she looks her sister in the eye and begins to speak slowly.
^This place gives me the creeps. I can't place my finger on it but everything about it just feels... off...
^Off as in it's weird, nothing makes sense, and everything seems to run on logic of its own. This is weird, and strange, and I don't like it.
^Okay, but you and I have seen strange things before; this is nothing new for us.
^Strange yes, but nothing like this. This is just weird; it's not natural.
^Constance feels her chest tighten. This can't be happening. She needs to stay strong, she has to. Who will protect Alice if she falls here?
^So what, this place doesn't follow the rules we usually use. We'll just learn them.
^Alice please, this isn't a game. This is real life, a life where actions have consequences. We can't just jump head first into everything.
^I'm not, I'm just saying that you don't have to panic over every little thing that's out of place. We can learn.
^Learn? How are we going to learn? By drinking more unknown bottles, by charging in recklessly without thinking first?
^Constance feels her frustration starting to rise and Alice can feel her calm slipping. Both girls are getting heated.
^I already told you, nothing happened. I feel fine, stop getting hung up on stupid details.
^Stupid? You could have died, you could have poisoned yourself, you could have gotten seriously hurt, but you didn't think, you never do! You always jump in without thinking things through.
^Look at us now, trapped in a strange world and you're still running around talking to strangers, telling them every little detail about yourself.
^That's not fair Con, you know he isn't going to hurt us.
^Do we? Because as far as I know we've known him for all of fifteen minutes and he's been giving off the most unsettling feeling.
^You always do that! You never believe in the good, always looking for the worst in a person.
^Oh, “always” huh? Well maybe if you learned to trust the people around you, it would help. Maybe then you wouldn't find yourself like this, frustrated and alone, with no one to turn to.
^Both girls are shaking now, anger clouding their judgement. Neither knows what to do here. This feels strange. They've had their fair share of fights but nothing like this.
^They stare at each other in tense silence before Constance breaks first.
^Whatever, I can't do this with you right now. Can we just go?
^Seriously we're leaving the conversation here? Aren't you going to say something back?
^I have nothing more to say. I just want to go home. So are you coming or not?
^Alice turns to look back at The Cat who stares intensely. She turns back to her sister who has a frown stretched across her face.
^I'm not going back there. I don't want to talk to him.
^But nothing Alice. Choose. Me or him. I'm going forward, and I hope to see you behind me when I turn around again.
^Her piece said, Constance begins to move forward along the path, but Alice feels rooted in place. Who is she supposed to choose here?

c-0: ^Alice turns back and walks towards The Cat. He appears to be happy with her choice!
c-1: ^Without a second thought Alice runs after her sister. She doesn't spare a glance at the angry cat scowling at her back.

ChooseCat: ^The Cat can't seem to hide the grin etched across his face, but Alice doesn't seem to notice it.
^Of course. I know my sister's being harsh… we can’t wait for her so I’ll just ask my questions first.
^Of course, ask away. I'm happy to lend a paw. Get it!?
^The Cat's cackle echoes through the silent forest.
^Well, uhhhh, where do I start? I've got so many questions for you.
^I mean it's my first time talking with a real cat, like an actual conversation.
^Hmmm, I suppose the concept itself feels quite revolutionary.
^Why don't you walk with me? We can chat while we walk, stretch our legs a little.
^I don't do well sitting still. Besides, if we walk together I can show you more things about this place.
^Alice's eyes seem to light up at the prospect of more Wonderland knowledge.
^And there are so many wonderful things I can show you.
^Alice appears conflicted. She wants to say yes and go with him, but her sister’s words still ring in the back of her mind.
^If it's Constance you're worried about, there's no need. I'll bring her to you after we're done.
^Alice thinks for a second before agreeing. She can always go back and look for her sister if she wants to.
^Alice takes off after him, eager to see what he has to offer.
ChooseConstance: ^Constance turns to watch her sister chase after her.
^I'm sorry, I didn't want to fight with you.
^No Alice, it's fine. I shouldn't have been so harsh with you.
^Still, you're always trying to protect me even when I don't get it. I should have known.
^I'm sorry for not listening to you earlier and I promise I'll do better.
^Constance feels an immense amount of guilt and gratitude for her kind sister.
^I know my curiosity always gets the better of me, and I'm really glad you're always looking out for me.
^I feel the same. I'm glad I have you here to keep me sane. I don't know what I would do without you.
^And for the record, I am sorry too, for yelling at you and the tugging. I was panicking but I shouldn't take it out on you.
^I know, I don't hold it against you.
^Still, I'm your big sister and it's my job to protect you, not hurt you. I promise I'll do better in the future.
^Both girls quickly hug, the feeling of warmth and happiness washing over them.
^Alright, what do you say we get out of here and go find the person in the woods.
^Sounds like an adventure! Count me in.
^With that, both girls take off down the pathway. A few meters back, a cheeky grin fades from view.
MadHatter: ^The girls look at the chaotic scene around them; it's a mess.
^There are cluttered tables, random items, and food scattered across the floor. It's gross and sticky.
^No idea, but I think he might know.
^Alice points to a man seated towards the back of the party. He appears to be in a heated argument with…nothing?
^Are you sure we should talk to him?
^Might as well. It's not like there's anyone else here.
^With that, both girls set off towards the table. As they near the man in question, he’s arguing passionately about something.
^I'm telling you, it's no use. It only tastes good with a bit of bubbles.
^What do you mean you want it plain? Why would anyone drink it like that?
^The girls watch in silence as the man argues with the air. He doesn't seem to notice either of them.
^I think we should wait. What if he's doing something important?
^Con, he's speaking to the air. Not sure how important that could be.
^Both girls watch as the man continues his silent argument.
^They stare at each other confused. What should they do here?

c-1: ^The girls decide to wait it out, but the man keeps going. Clearly he's on a roll.
^Alice starts to get impatient. Constance can tell Alice is at her wits end.
^I suppose we should just ask him now, I don't think you have the patience to wait.

Choice_5_FollowUp: ^The Mad Hatter pauses his conversation. He looks at both girls, perplexed.
^Mad Hatter huh, that's a fun name!
^I'm sorry about this. We didn't mean to interrupt your conversation.
^Some think it’s a funny name, I quite like it. It's important to me.
^Of course it's important to you, it's your name, it makes you... well, uhhh you!
^The Mad Hatter gives her a funny look, it appears both sad and amused by her statement
^Yes, well I suppose you're right. In that case let me hear yours. After all, names are important.
^You’re absolutely right! I'm Alice! And this is my sister. It's nice to meet you.
^Constance levels her sister a glare, but she can't do anything about it now.
^I'm Constance, it's a pleasure to meet you.
^Mad Hatter seemingly breaks out of his mind fog.
^The pleasure's all mine! It's not everyday you see a new face around here.
^You're the second person to say that today. Where even is here?
^It's Wonderland silly, I've told you this.
^Your sister speaks the truth. This place is Wonderland, a place filled with endless joy and wonder.
^He smiles, though his eyes don't hold it.
^A place filled with warmth, a place filled with true happiness, a place called home!
^He's shouting now, rambling, and his sentences seem to turn to nonsense.
^Is that not the case my friend? This place is a miracle!
^To the girls, only silence fills the area, but the Hatter seems satisfied with the answer he receives.
^Of course, it's alright. When have we ever gotten trouble from him? He leaves us alone at this point.
^There is nothing left for us, she watches as she pleases and we let her, she is our guard.
^Uhhh Mr. Mad Hatter? We want to ask for directions. Would you know… uh…
^The question stops the man’s rant. Clearly his mind is elsewhere.
^Both girls look equally shocked. He's already forgotten them?
^Uhhh I'm Alice? And this is my sister-
^Yes of course, how could I have forgotten, are you here for a cup of tea?
^We have the finest tea here. No one else can compete. We're truly the best!
^We didn't come here for tea, we came-
^You don't look like you're from around here? Are you new?
^Yeah we came from a rabbit hole.
^A rabbit hole! Truly fascinating. What might this hole look like?
^Well, it was big and round, and covered in pretty flowers-
^Flowers, yes of course, they’re important, though I can’t remember why…
^Yes of course you gather  flowers. You gather them for… For tea time! Oh my friends, the hour is upon us.
^Yes of course, 6pm sharp! You can’t be late.
^Was it the red ones, or the purple ones?
^The Mad Hatter quickly grows frustrated, unable to remember what he needed.
^He begins to mumble to himself. ‘Missing ingredients,’ is all he says.
^Yes I must make something, he needs it. We've planned this for ages…or was it yesterday.
^Something washes over the Mad Hatter's face. A wave of sorrow and guilt. It's quickly replaced by a mask of indifference.
^Yes, my friend needs it. I promised him I would make tea.
^Ohhh we could help you make tea. Constance makes the best teas at home.
^Maybe another time Alice. We need to keep moving.
^Are you in a hurry?  Relax, sit down, it’s tea time after all.
^Yes, we’re in a hurry. We were told someone here can help us find a Queen. Someone who might, in turn, help us leave this place.
^At this the Mad Hatter freezes, the joy on his face slipping slightly.
^The Queen you say… well! That's an interesting goal you have there.
^She does have a way to uhh "help,” I suppose.
^Do you know her well? Could you help us get to her?
^I mean, I know her as well as anyone else here. She's our Queen, after all.
^Something about The Mad Hatter’s tone feels off.
^You hear him? You can really hear it?
^He appears rattled by this new revelation.
^Yeah, I mean it's not hard. You gotta learn to tune it out sometimes, though, because it can be annoying.
^The Mad Hatter looks terrified by this new information. He backs away from the girls, his expression reminding them of a deer trapped in headlights.
^So Mr. Hatter, can you help us?
^Oh for goodness sake! Help us get to the Queen to go back home.
^Yeah…I mean, well. I’m pretty content here, but Con wants to leave.
^I’m sorry, what? You want to stay?
^It’s been fun so far! Better than reading, that’s for sure.
^But knowing you, you're probably stressing about all the math tests and clarinet lessons you’re missing.
^This place could be covered in gold for all I care, I would still want to go home. Plus, it's only been like… a day. My math test is next week.
^These “math tests” sound strange. Perhaps they’re some creature you dispose of? Our Queen might know, yes she always knows what to do. But she can’t help you. She won’t help either of you..
^What? But why? The Cheshire Cat told us she could help.
^At The Cat's name, the Mad Hatter pales.
^He- he sent you here? To me?
^Something like that. He’s not the most straight forward if you know what I mean.
^A wounded noise is pulled from the Ma Hatter’s mouth. The man is unravelling quickly.
^He gestures wildly at the air, throwing accusations and questions sometimes nodding along like he's receiving an answer.
^Eventually whatever he was talking to manages to calm him down enough to turn back to the girls.
^He wants me to help you. I don't want to help, but you were sent here. Maybe there was a reason. A chance to earn your place.
^Tea. I need help making my teas.I keep forgetting which ingredients to collect, I think the list is somewhere he was always better at keeping our notes organized.
^So we need to grab ingredients to make tea for a ticket? What’s the ticket for?
^It’s a ticket to the Queen's Party today. If you help me I’ll give them to you.
^Really? That’s awesome! What do we do first?
^The Mad Hatter begins to space off once more. He snaps back after a second.
^Yes you did, you wanted us to make it, so tell us what type you want.
^I uhh, whatever is fine, there's a list and a recipe book somewhere here. He wrote it down so I wouldn't forget but now I can’t remember where I put it.
^Do you want us to get it for-
^Yes, yes just do whatever you must do. I must go now there’s something I must do. At least I think I do… Come back when you’ve found everything.
^And with that he turns away from the girls.
^Well you heard the man. Off we go to find a list and make some tea. I think I saw the list!
^I’ll grab the recipe book. For the love of god, this better be worth it...
MadHatterWin: ^How? How did you know that recipe? I've been trying for ages but I never got it right?
^Well I didn't do it alone, this helped!
^Constance shows him the book she picked up earlier.
^His eyes appear clear for the first time, though tears prick the corners.
^We found it in the mess here, it was just lying around.
^I see... Of course it was, he was always doing that, leaving his things all over the place.
^Well I suppose this makes sense. He was always good at making my favorite tea.
^Who was he? The book says it belongs to someone by the name March Hare?
^He was my best friend, the greatest person I ever met.
^And I will cherish him, till the day I die.
^The Mad Hatter looks wistfully at the sky.
^Where is he now?? Can we meet him?
^...He's not here at the moment, he's somewhere else, a little too far for me to reach...
^Awwww, that sucks. I hope you see him soon.
^I hope to see him as well, I hope I can see him soon.
^The smile that graces his face feels genuine. He looks at peace holding the book in his hands.
^I don't have the words to thank you for this. I truly don't.
^You don't have to. I'm glad we could return it to you safe and sound.
^Still, you have no idea what this means to me. I will forever be in your debt.
^It's fine silly, just be happy you got your book back!
^...Thank you. Truly from the bottom of my heart, thank you!
^A moment of silence stretches between them.
^Oh my how silly of me, I almost forgot!
^He produces an invitation from his pocket and hands it to Constance.
^Take it, it's yours. It's the least I can do after what you've done for me.
^I- I don't know what to say.
^All three look at one another, soft smiles stretch across their faces.
^You should hurry, you need to make it to the castle before time runs out.
^Follow the pathway forward and it should lead you to your next destination.
^Do not stray from the path, you are one step closer to home!
^Taking the warning in stride both girls begin to make their way down the path.
^BYE MISTER MAD HATTER. THANK YOU FOR THE TEA!
^You are most welcome my dear Alice! I hope victory shall find you first!
^With that in mind the girls make their way down the worn path, hopeful they'll make it home.
MadHatterLose: ^You call yourselves tea makers? How shameful.
^We did no such thing. You asked for our help!
^Well some help you were! You couldn't even make a decent tea.
^Hey! We tried our best, it's not our fault your instructions suck!
^A true tea artist would be able to tell what the client wants from their soul.
^That doesn't even make sense. Don't try to justify your poor instructions with nonsense.
^How dare you! Nonsense, I'll have you know even the most basic of tea makers can decipher what I needed.
^But it doesn't matter now, it's too late and tea time is ruined.
^The Mad Hatter sulks, clearly unhappy with the results.
^I have nothing else to say to either of you. Leave, I don't want to see you here.
^Well that makes two of us pal. No need to be so rude!
^Alice storms off, and Constance follows after her,
^She turns to look at the Mad Hatter once more but he disappears from view just as quickly as he came.
^Pushing the memory out of her mind she runs after her sister instead.
WhiteRabbitHouse: ^The girls continue down the path forward. Alice rushing in front and Constance trailing behind.
^Alice, wait up, you're moving too quickly.
^The girls fall into a quiet stretch, the sounds of labored breathing fill the air around them.
^Suddenly Alice spots movement to the right and freezes.
^The bunny? I saw the bunny again?
^The bunny that got us into this mess??
^Yeah!! I'm pretty sure it's the same one.
^Alice looks delighted while Constance feels annoyed.
^Alice please don't go chasing the rabbit again...
^And with that Alice takes off rushing after the rabbit. Constance curses under her breath and follows.
^Both girls give chase for a while before finally the pathway clears and a large house comes into view.
^A house? What's it doing here in the middle of the woods?
^Alice quickly runs forward to meet the rabbit in question, Constance following close.
^Hi!!! How are you? My name's Alice. What's yours?
^Is this your house? Do you live here?
^Alice please, one question at a time you're going to overwhelm him.
^A moment of silence stretches between them.
^Who are you? And how did you find this place?
^I'm Alice! And this here is sister Constance.
^I see... It's lovely to meet the both of you. Now if you'll excuse me I have places to be.
^Alice don't bother the man, he's clearly busy.
^The rabbit is already leaving and Alice runs after him ignoring her sister's warning.
^Please just five minutes! I just want to know more about you. I saw you before,
^Near the rabbit hole. You dropped a watch on your way down!
^No, that's not possible you couldn't have been there.
^Sure I could have! I saw you before, when we were sitting under the oak tree.
^You kept fishing out that watch and looking at it before running.
^And then when you jumped down the hole I'm pretty sure it fell out.
^No, no, no, that's- that's not true, it simply can't be true.
^The White Rabbit appears to be shaken by this new information.
^I'm really sorry for my sister's actions. I don't know what she was thinking.
^And I know we're being bothersome right now but we were hoping you could help us find someone.
^Yes, we're looking for the Queen. We were told she could help us find our way home.
^What??? Who- who told you that? The Queen??
^No, the queen didn't tell us anything we haven't met her.
^What my sister means is that we met someone who told us to go see her.
^And they told you, that the Queen, our Queen, would help you get home.
^The White Rabbit looks extremely conflicted by this but says nothing.
^Yeah something like that, I can't remember what he said exactly but he told us about her.
^So can you help us? Find the Queen?
^I- I'm not so sure. I don't think the Queen will want to meet today.
^Yes she's extremely busy she has things to do- OH MY!
^The girls watch as the White Rabbit remembers something and begins to rush towards his home.
^I must go, I simply cannot stay here. The Queen is waiting for me to return.
^So you know the Queen? Can you help us?
^I can but not right now, I have to find them quickly, I must get to the castle quickly!
^Find what? Are you in a rush to go?
^Yes, I must grab my things quickly. The Queen doesn't like tardiness.
^Maybe we can help? It will be faster if we help you look for things.
^The Rabbit freezes at this, before turning to look at both girls.
^Nothing. We were hoping you could help us locate the Queen in exchange for our help.
^The White Rabbit appears deep in thought for a moment. Eventually his face settles into something neutral
^Very well, I may need your help. I only have a half hour before I have to leave.
^Great, what do you need from us?
^I am trusting you with my home, please do not go around snooping, only grab what's on the list in the lobby.
^Of course! We'll be careful, we promise!
^Constance eyes her sister but says nothing aloud to hopefully ease the White Rabbit
^...Very well. The list is in the lobby, please gather my items. I will pack the remainder of my tools in my study.
^We will meet in the lobby in a half hour. Try not to be late.
^With that the White Rabbit shuffles away, leaving the two girls to themselves.
^We divide and conquer. There's too many rooms to search together.
^Okay I like the sound of that. You take two items and I take two.
^Yes, then we'll meet back here in a bit.
^Alright see you in a bit sis!
WhiteRabbitPuzzle: ^Alice sees the White Rabbit sitting in the chair by himself. He looks haggard.
^Hello Alice. What are you doing here?
^I got a little turned around. Sorry about that.
^Simply taking a moment to myself, it's not easy at my age.
^Now that's quite the impolite question young lady.
^Oops, sorry. I didn't mean it like that.
^Alice looks down embarrassed as the White Rabbit chuckles.
^Well it's not like I'm offended by the question. Still you should be careful, you never know who you'll meet.
^Hmmm, I'll keep that in mind. Con's always telling me to watch my mouth.
^Your sister's right, one of these days it's going to get you in trouble.
^Ehhh I'll deal with it if I ever get to that point.
^Oh my, you're quite the free spirit. I suppose it's refreshing to see someone as optimistic as you down here.
^Alice can tell the topic is sensitive, should she push?

c-0: ^I don't get it? Why's everyone so shifty around here?
^The White Rabbit appears tense, perhaps this was the wrong choice...
^You should be careful Alice. Don't go sticking your nose in places it doesn't belong,
^But why? No one ever gives a straight answer here. It's always wrapped up in twisted riddles.
^Because that's how it works down here. Nothing is free, everything comes with a price.
^Even this? Do you want something from me, for sitting down and talking to you?
^Alice can feel herself getting upset at the thought. She liked the old man.
^I wouldn't want your things, I doubt a little girl such as yourself even has anything to offer.
^HEY! I'm plenty smart, Con says I'm old enough to do school work on my own.
^... that is not what I meant. This conversation is leading nowhere, perhaps it's time you leave.
c-1: ^Alice decides to leave the conversation alone, the mood seems to lighten.

Alice_choices: ^I have more questions though. I thought you would answer them.
^The White Rabbit stays silent for a moment before deciding.
^Fine, ask your questions but please be quick about it. I don't have all day.
^Ask about his relationship with the Queen

c-0: ^What else is there? She is our Queen, we watch over her as she watches us.
^But why? Everyone we've talked to either talks about her like she's a god or a nuisance.
^Please, don't ever say that out loud, never! You don't know who could be listening.
^Just please, trust me this once, the Queen is not someone to be trifled with.
^She is as powerful as the whispers and she rules these lands.
^If she rules these lands then why does everyone hold a sense of sadness for them.
^That is something I cannot answer. Please do not ask me again...
^Alice wants to push forward but she feels it won't do anything. Back to the drawing board.
c-1: ^This is Wonderland... a place of wonder and joy.
^Everyone says that, it's this amazing place filled with possibilities.
^So why does it feel like no one truly believes that?
^Everywhere we go it feels like people are missing parts of themselves, like important bits have been taken.
^Ahhh children, always looking for puzzles that don't exist.
^It means not everything is a complicated picture you need to put back together, sometimes simplicity is beauty.
^And that's what this place is, simple?
^This place holds beauty in its rules, it keeps order and maintains this paradise.
^You will learn little one, and when you do I hope we don't teach you that lesson.
^He leaves Alice confused but it appears the White Rabbit has no more answers as he is checked out now.
c-2: ^What about The Cheshire Cat? What's your relationship with him?
^That mangy scoundrel, I don't ever want to talk about him again.
^More than anything in this world. I will never forgive that creature.
^But, he seemed so nice? He even helped us find the Hatter and get the ticket.
^Make no mistake, that thing doesn't care, he only does what he pleases.
^It doesn't matter who he hurts, it's all a game to him in the end.
^And has he hurt you? Is that why you're angry?
^The White Rabbit's face goes cold. He does not answer the question. This topic has come to a close.
c-3: ^What about this? Do you recognize them?
^The White Rabbit stares at the locket shining in Alice's palm.
^I found it, it was inside a room. A baby room?
^I didn't mean to snoop, I just saw it was on the list and thought you might want it.
^So, you figured it out. I didn't think you would to be honest.
^Yes, they're the most precious people in my life. At least they were until that thing took them away.
^Yes. I feel angry, and hurt and pained.
^They sit in the silence, Alice doesn't know how to fix this.
^Is this why you work for her?
^...yes. She promised, she said she would help me get them back.
^What choice did I have? It's the only option left.
^There's always an option, you just have to look for it.
^Spoken like a true child, one that still believes the good in this world.
^The world is not as kind to you when you grow to understand it.
^Is that why you chose to lock it away? Lock them up along with a piece of your heart?
^The reason you chose to serve her, even if it kills you inside.
^Yes, I pushed it away, locked it somewhere I could never touch so this pain would no longer hurt me.
^I chose this path, because it was all I had left, all I had to numb the hurt.
^And I hoped I could keep this piece locked away in the only place that has meaning here.
^The white rabbit gazes sadly at the locket in her palm. It feels heavy, like she shouldn't have it.
^The White Rabbit holds her hand in silence. Their eyes never leave the locket.

continue_story: ^After a moment of silence the White Rabbit speaks once more.
^Yeah, that's all I wanted to ask.
^Well I hope I satisfied your curiosity one way or another.
^I still don't know why you bothered to help me or answer my questions.
^I- it just felt, like the right thing to do.
^Nevermind that, we're running out of time. You should hurry and help your sister.
^Will I be able to come back? If I have more questions.
^I will be here, as long as I have time, I will sit here.
^Alice nods realizing the conversation is over.
ThePoster: ^The girls finally reconvene in the lobby. What should they do now?
^As the girls wonder what to do next, Alice spots a poster hanging on the wall.
^You think! I would have noticed something that creepy by now.
^Creepy? It doesn't look creepy. Maybe a little dreamy but not creepy.
^Are you kidding me?? Look at her face, that's like the face of evil right there.
^Evil? But she looks so serene here?
^Do your eyes work? What are you talking about?
^Before the girls can argue more the White Rabbit rushes in clearly frazzled.
^You girls ready to leave? We're going to be late if we don't leave now.
^The Rabbit rushes to greet them only to see the poster in question and freeze.
^Uhhh not sure, we just saw it now. Con says it wasn't here when we came in.
^That rat, that no good conniving snake! Of course, he would come here just to ruin my day?
^Both Alice and Constance are shocked by the outburst in question.
^That thing, it just wants me to suffer. That's what this is isn't it? You want to see me cry?
^Maybe you want me to beg, plead, tell you to leave me alone. BUt I WILL NEVER BREAK FOR YOU.
^The Rabbit runs around the house, shouting into corners about standing strong against these attacks.
^The girls both look at each other in confusion.
^I'm just gonna go- and like get him.
^Okay, yeah you go do that. I'll just stay here I guess.
^Alice takes off after the White Rabbit and leaves Constance in the silence. Though that doesn't last long.
^Mannnn that old thing really doesn't know when to give up.
^Rude, first of all I have a name. Second, I go wherever I please.
^I- that doesn't explain why you're here now?
^It doesn't? Oh well it doesn't really matter to me, I just thought it would be fun.
^To mess with that old fool. He really doesn't know when to quit.
^That's no way to treat your elder!
^HAHAHAAHAH. Elder?! Oh my that's a good one.
^Little girl, you have no idea what I am or what I can do. Don't give me a lecture.
^No. A promise. One that could show you more. If you're curious that is.
^The answers, the ones you seek so desperately.
^My, you really are so quick to judge. I just don't see the need to fuss? Do you want answers or not?
^Nothing in this world is free. Everything comes at a cost.
^I suppose that's true... well I guess all I want for you to do is follow me through the poster.
^Give me a sense of closure, knowing someone finally took the chance to learn the truth.
^Think about it. There's a world full of answers just waiting for you. All it takes is one step forward!
^Yes! One step forward and all the troubles you've had will wash away.
^A war wages inside Constance. What is she to do here?

c-0: ^Constance decides to follow The Cat through the poster. Somehow this feels easier than it should.
c-1: ^Constance decides to trust her gut and stay behind. The Cat appears annoyed by the choice.
^Before he can comment, they hear Alice and the White Rabbit returning.
^Well I suppose that's my cue to leave.
^Of course, it's your loss sweetie, I told you I could show you something amazing!
^Constance only gives the thing a scowl as it disappears into the painting once more.

b: ^Alice and the Rabbit come back just as The Cat leaves. They appear calmer now.
^Alice and the Rabbit come back just as The Cat leaves. They appear calmer now.

WhiteRabbitWin: ^Apologies for the outburst, I'm quite calm now.
^No worries, I'm glad you feel better now!
^Yes, but we don't have time to dwell on that, we're quite late as is.
^The girls give the White Rabbit his things as they rush out the door.
^Quickly follow me now, we don't have a moment to spare.
^With that the White Rabbit quickly bolted into the woods, the girls rushing to follow.
WhiteRabbitLose: ^Apologies for the outburst, I'm quite calm now.
^No worries, I'm glad you feel better now!
^Yes, but we don't have time to dwell on that, we're quite late as is.
^I'm sorry we weren't able to gather all your things...
^Oh, I see. Well that's quite unfortunate. I suppose we don't really have the time to dwell on the matter.
^With that said the White Rabbit simply turns and rushes out the door without waiting for the girls.
^It's not like we have much of a choice, we don't know where to go!
^With that the girls bolted out the door desperate to find the White Rabbit's trail.
TheGate: ^The girls watch as the White Rabbit quickly disappears out of view. The forest gives way to a towering castle.
^We need to hurry, we're going to lose the Rabbit.
^They reach the Gate and two guards stand strong, checking people's IDs.
^Ahh, hello sir, the Queen is waiting for you.
^Hold on, not you. You need to present an ID to get in.
^The girls are confused at first but quickly realize they need to do something before they get locked out.

c-0: ^The guards are suspicious of both girls. They take the invite and look closely.
^The girls excitedly rush in. The White Rabbit is long gone so they rush to catch up.
c-1: ^Yes, both girls are with me. Let them in once you check them.
^With that the White Rabbit disappears through the large gate.
^Alright girls you got any weapons or harmful objects on you?
^What she means is, thank you! And have a nice day.
^Constance grabs her sister and bolts through the door, the White Rabbit is nowhere to be seen.
c-2: ^The guard stares at you expectantly. You have nothing to offer.
^The Rabbit has gone through the door and there is no invite.
^If you could just give us a moment of your time maybe we could-
^There are no exceptions to the rule. If you don't have ID you cannot enter.
^Ever stands silently staring at each other. Eventually Constance breaks and grabs her sister.
^We'll find another way in. Let's go. There's nothing here for us.
c-3: ^Are you sure this is a good idea?
^I mean it's not like we can do anything else.
^Maybe there's another way inside. Let's take a look.

QueenCastle: ^Stay close. We don't want to get lost here.
^As the girls approach the opening of what appears to be a very large maze they see a card panicking.
^Oh no, no no no. This can't be happening… not again.
^I'm Alice, and this is my sister Constance.
^Are you feeling alright? You're sweating so much!
^No, everything is terrible. The gardeners messed up again and now it's my head on the chopping block.
^The poor card appears to be in tears.
^The roses, they were to plant red roses to honor our Queen today, but some fool went and planted white ones.
^The Red Queen cannot have WHITE roses in her garden. It's blasphemous.
^It is! If we don't do something the whole party will end in ruins.
^Fine. If it makes you feel better we can take a look and grab the white ones off the hedges.
^Yeah, let's do it. We have to go through the maze to get in anyway.
^You would do that. For me? Oh thank you! Thank you!!!
^You can save your thanks for now. Wait until we get all the roses first.
^Of course, of course. I wish you luck!
^With that the girls quickly took off into the maze.

b: ^Alice and Constance make their way out of the maze. All the white roses sit in their hands.
^Hopefully this should stop the card from getting in trouble.
^Yeah I hope so too. Quick let's go find them.
^Alice and Constance are panicking, the Queen is coming and they've yet to grab all the roses.
^Oh thank goodness you made it out. Were you able to grab all the roses?
^No, we didn't. We  ran out of time.
^At this the card looks scared. He's shaking as the Queen approaches them.
^Seven, what is going on here? Why are people congregating at the maze?
^Nothing, your majesty. These girls were just leaving.
^He says grabbing both girls arms and pulling them forward. The Queen's eyes drift towards the maze.
^The Queen plucks a white rose out of a bush, it's light petals shining in the fading sun.
^My Queen I can assure you that-
^But nothing. You dare sit there and deceive me. Plant roses that oppose me in my own garden!
^Please my Queen, if you would just hear me out.
^I have no time for the likes of you! Guards drag him away. Lock him with the others.
^The card begs and pleads to no avail, it's dragged away looking shaken and scared.
^And you two. What do you have to do with this?
^Nothing, your royal highness. We've come today to seek a meeting with you.
^Yes, we would like to discuss and urgent matter with you, if you could lend us some of your time-
^If you wish to use my time, why must you insult me in the same breath.
^The girls look at each other puzzled.
^That girl over there, don't think I'm blind. I see those roses stuffed in your pocket.
^At this both girls freeze. The white flowers sitting in Alice's dress now feel heavy.
^This won't do. No this won't do at all. People will think I've gone soft.
^Your majesty if you could just give us a moment-
^SILENCE! I shall make an example out of you two. Guards drag them to court!
^We will show everyone what happens when you choose to defy me.
^Both girls are helpless as the guards descend and drag them off to court.

GardenPartyTalk: ^Thank you so much for helping us. I don't know what the Red Queen would have done to us or you.
^To you? To us? The queen wouldn’t…
^Constance chooses to ignore the remark in favor of asking the card a couple questions.
^Mr. Seven of Hearts, can I ask you about something? It’s about some weird stuff in the maze. It just reminded me of an article I found saying awful things about -
^Attention, All Garden Party Guests, Your Majesty, The Red Queen, Saviour, Beacon of Brilliance has arrived!
^The Red Queen strides down from her glamorous castle. She acts like her presence is a blessing on this party.
^Con! She's coming this way. What do we do?
^The Red Queen walks up next to the rose maze.
^Mr. Seven of Hearts, wonderful job on the hedge trimming.
^Thank you! I take your compliment with gratitude!
^First thing you’ve done correctly in all your card forsaken life.
^Well! Keep this up, Mr Seven. I despise disappointment. You sevens should know this by now.
^After leveling the man with a glare that could kill, the Queen turns to her party guests and smiles.
^Well then, with this sorted, the Queen is ready to mingle! Let the music commence.
^Um, excuse me, miss - Her majesty, The Red Queen. I need to speak to you about something.
^Did I give you permission to speak to me? What is it now??
^I cannot in good faith take all the praise. These girls assisted in the maze clean up, all I ask is that you give them a moment of your time..
^WHO ARE YOU TO TELL ME WHAT TO DO WITH MY TIME!?
^The queen promptly turns away from Mr. Seven of Hearts to the girls.
^But your majesty. These girls fought viciously to free your garden of those pesky white flowers. Look, not a single flower remains.
^At this the Queen’s beady little eyes scan the outer maze. True to the card’s words it’s finally devoid of white.
^Then I must offer you my thanks. Oh my sweets. I apologize for the outburst.
^Bless his heart, Mr. Seven doesn't know his place. Anyway, I really do appreciate you both for helping with the trimming.
^The Queen, basking in all her glory, smiles upon the garden party. She thinks its “brilliance” is only rivaled by her own.
^Your Majesty, your party, it’s very…decadent and exquisite! It seems like everyone loves you.
^Ahh, they do. My humble subjects. Each and every one of them.
^Can I ask you about some of them?
^Well ask. Hurry up, The Queen doesn’t have all day.
Alice_Queen_Choices: ^Ask about their relationship with the Rabbit

c-0: ^What is your connection to Mr. Rabbit?
^Oh, he’s just around. Helping run errands. He looks old, but is as energetic as a rabbit! I guess he is a rabbit. I've never realized that before. HA HA!
^It seems like he waits on you, hand and foot.
^He is my right-hand man after all.
^Of course, this party was organized by him. Though every detail was hand picked by your’s truly!
^Alice feels sick, she tries hard to swallow down the bile and focus on the woman in front of her.
^Why does this cut deeper than the others?
c-1: ^Ah what a wonderful place. My sweet, sweet home. Did you know I was born to be Queen? Truly a heroic feat. And I did it myself.
^Out of all the Queens, I am the best one. Queen of Spades does nothing all day but whine and cry. Clearly, she’s dealt a hand of misery.
^My kingdom has celebrations all the time! I mean, look at the garden party. I am a great Ruler-.
^Well I was hoping to hear your thoughts on the people of Wonderland.
^Oh! Well yes! I mean, this party is for them after all. Mostly…
c-2: ^We do not SPEAK THAT NAME IN THIS KINGDOM.
^I do not want to participate in this conversation. If we keep talking about this, I will throw you in the dungeon! OFF WITH -
^Good. Fine. See, I am a very forgiving person. HA HA!

continue_story_2: ^Constance pulls out the newspaper clipping she wasn’t supposed to find at the White Rabbit’s house.
^I- I know I shouldn’t read it but the things in here are strange. I’m honestly not sure what to believe anymore.
^Hush, child, here is not the place to discuss this. Come with me now.
GardenPartyPuzzle: ^The Queen drags Constance to a secluded place inside the castle.
^The silver of kindness she once had seems to have disappeared or maybe her face always looked like that.
^Ummm, you're majesty, where exactly are you going?
^Her voice is harsh, maybe Constance is finally going to see beneath the mask?
^Please I just, I didn't realize it was a sensitive topic.
^I- I never said you were. Please don't misunderstand me.
^I just can't stand that thing, that pesky little rat!
^Oh please, don't act like you haven't met that mutt.
^I assure you I have no idea what you're talking about. I just found this-
^Oh yes of course, you just found a piece of paper made out to mock me and brought it up at my own party.
^I'm sure you meant the best, isn't that right sweetheart?
^Constance is cringing internally, probably wasn't the best idea to bring that up in a crowd of people.
^I do apologize for my timing. I wasn't thinking clearly and I should have been more tactful.
^And I don't mean to attack you in any way. I was just curious about the origins of this piece.
^Yes, do you believe what's written here? Or are you going to pretend like you weren't plotting against me?

c-0: ^No I don't believe it, not even a bit. That's why I came to you.
^Yes I wanted to know your side of the story. After all there are two sides to a story.
^I suppose that was quite gracious of you. Most people wouldn't have sought out the truth.
^Constance stuck to her bold-faced lie. She doesn't seem to care what she needs to do, she just wants the truth.
^If you must know it's all because of that stupid pest. Him and that stupid grin.
^DON'T SPEAK THAT THINGS NAME IN MY PRESENCE
^Yikes, it seems the Queen's flipped her lid again.
^I apologize. I will keep that in mind. Please continue...
^Yes, well, it's his fault this happened in the first place.
^Here I am doing my best to rule this kingdom and what does this flea do?
^Write a spoof piece discrediting all the work you've done?
^YES! Exactly he writes all these horrible lies that make me look like a manipulative fool.
^I mean it really hurt my feelings...
^I'm sorry that happened. I know it must feel terrible to watch someone close lie to you.
^Close? Oh heaven's no, that thing isn't allowed within 50ft of my castle.
^I couldn't care less about something. It's only job seems to be getting in my way and tormenting me at any given turn.
^By writing bad new articles about you?
^By spreading lies! Poisoning my soldiers against me.
^Do you think he has a reason to do that?
^My sweet, that stupid cat doesn't need a reason to do anything. He does what he wants without a care for others.
^What an ironic statement. It almost feels like she should be looking in a mirror and telling herself that.
^I just, please at least tell me there's no merit to what he claims here. That it's all baseless lies.
^Of course it's a lie. I am the QUEEN. What I say goes, and if you don't like it the door is that way.
^The Queen seems completely done with this conversation, was it time to stop pushing?
^Which one of you guards is responsible for letting this nuisance run around my castle unmonitored.
^Alice appears unfazed by the comment and barrels forward.
^Man am I glad to see you!
^Yeah of course I was, you just disappeared outta nowhere!
^Well we were in a rush. I didn't have time to warn you.
^Warn me about what? Everyone saw her drag you away. Had to fight the guards to let me in.
^Of all the disrespect! How dare you, come into my home and resort to violence.
^Oh like you're one to talk! What happened to Mr.Seven
^What happens to my guards is none of your concern!
^It is when you're hurting him. He didn't do anything wrong.
^Such insolence. How dare you speak to me that way. I am THE QUEEN.
^You could be the Queen of the World and it still wouldn't matter to me. What gives you the right to treat people like that?
^I can do whatever I want, I am the Queen, and that means everything is under my command.
^That includes you. GUARDS! Take the girl to the cellar, maybe it will give her some time to cool off.
^Constance doesn't know why but something compels her to stand up for her sister.
^You heard me. Take me instead of her.
^You would do that for her? Why?
^Because she's my sister, and I love her.
^You speak as if you are holding yourself to a high standard.
^I'm the older one. It's always gonna be my job.
^Very well, I suppose I will honor your wish.
^No! No don't do this, please Con don't do this.
^Stop panicking Alice. I'll be out in a bit, you'll see. Just keep out of trouble okay.
^Constance is led away by a group of guards, her sister screaming for them to stop and give her sister back.
c-1: ^I'm not sure anymore. I don't know what to think.
^No- of course not. I'm just confused...
^Confused about what, either you are with me or you are against me. Which is it?
^Constance feels confused by this statement. The situation at hand wasn't so black and white.
^I am neither for you or against you. I merely want to hear the truth from you.
^The only truth that matters is that I am right and you are wrong.
^That's not the truth, no one is ever always right.
^How dare you! I'm always right, I am the Queen, the ruler, the highest among you mortals.
^So what? That doesn't make you any less human.
^I followed you since she grabbed you. Took me a second to get past the guards but I'm here.
^I don't see how this will help you in any way. I'm still right.
^Just because you say you're correct doesn't mean you are. If you can't acknowledge your faults then you've failed.
^A queen cannot fail, she rules above all else, with purpose and reason. Nothing will break her spirit.
^But even a good queen can acknowledge when they have caused pain and done wrong.
^Are you insinuating that I am a bad queen?
^Arghhh, do you even listen? She's saying that you aren't taking accountability for anything you said or did.
^Your only defence is that you are the Queen and what you say goes. That isn't a defence that's oppression.
^The Queen's face twists into something ugly and horrific.
^You come to my home, tarnish my party and sully my reputation.
^We didn't do any of that, all I wanted was the answer to
^And I gave you an answer. Yet you still questioned me. ME!?
^Being a queen doesn't give you a pass to be awful to people.
^I will not be spoken to like this, not be mere- children.
^Fine then let the others decide. Let them see who's right and who's wrong.
^A battle of wits! Both sides will present their case, and we'll have people from the crowd participate.
^You give us a chance to speak and you listen to what we have to say. Really listen.
^And you can use this as an opportunity to show your subjects how fair you are as a Queen.
^The Queen's eyes light up with a unique kind of greed.
^...Very well. I shall accept your challenge. We shall duel to the death!
^Escort these young ladies to the court, we must prepare for the duel.
^The girls watch in horror as the Queen waltzes off in a daze ordering people about.

QueenCourt: ^The girls are dragged up to the stand and deposited in the box. The room fills quickly as the cards leave them.
^Oh man look at all the people...
^Just stay calm, don't panic,  we're going to be fine.
^The girls lapse into silence as they watch faces new and familiar fill the stand. The Rabbit walks quickly without looking at either of them.
^Quiet, please! Court will be in session now. All rise for her honorable judge and ruler The Red Queen.
^The room falls into an uneasy hush as the Queen takes her place on the stand, she's exudes enough power to command the room.
^Y-yes of course. We gather here today to witness the trial of Alice and Constance two girls who came to challenge the Queen's authority and undermine her influence.
^Gasps are heard among the crowd, the place was going to get rowdy!
^Their- crimes include: Disrespecting our beloved ruler, questioining her authority, undermining her decisions and destorying her party with their foul attuides.
^Con did you hear what he's saying, it's utter nonsense-
^QUIET! YOU WILL GET YOUR TURN TO SPEAK.
^The buzzing in the room fades, everyone sits silent their stares pinning both girls down.
^As I was saying... we now gather here today to here their story, and decide what punishment is apporiate for crimes of this magnintude.
^You here that, they've already decided we're guilty before we've started.
^What are we supposed to do, the whole thing is rigged.
^Fight, like we always do, you logic and reasoning. When the truth is on your side you will always prevail.
^For the first time Alice feels a wave of relief wash over her. They were both here, fighting that's all that mattered.
^Let us start. We will go down the list of your crimes, who they pertain to and you will be given a chance to defend yourself. Use it wisely...
^Charge One: Guilty of Blantent disrespect against our monarch.
^Correct, I opened my arms to these girls, let them in, took the time to show them around and this is the thanks I get.
^Alice your actions at the party were cruel and unjust. You cornered our Queen, forced question upon question on her and misrepresented her attitude. How do you plead?
^Not guilty! I didn't force her to do anything!
^Did you not ask me questions related to the way I rule my people? I'm certain several partygoers heard you.

c-0: ^The crowd gasps, startled by Alice's bluntness.
^However you act like my questions were malicious. I was curious about this world and it's people, so I asked the one person who should know about them.
^If the answers you gave made you feel bad that's not a refelction on me but yourself.
^You can't expect people to respect you when all you do is flaunt yourself to the world.
^You need to learn to let others in. It can't always revolve around you.
^I am the Queen, the world does revolve around me.
^Then you'll stay stuck where you are. Someone who's unable to admit their own faults will never learn to move ahead.
^I learned that lesson the hard way from someone very dear to me-
^Spare me the sob story, I have no use listening to those things. Next sentence.
^Tensions are rising, Alice feels condifient boosting Constance's moral.
c-1: ^No? You're going to act like you didn't accuse me of hurting my own people?
^I didn't say it like that, stop twisting my words.
^But you admit that you did say something!
^Do you hear that! She admits, she has set me up from the start.
^The crowd's roar of anger drowns Alice's response. She watches helplessly as they move to the next item.
^We move to the second crime. The crime of disrespecting the Queen's decision. In this case Constance set the Queen up, ambushing her with a banned article and opening her up to public scrutiny.
^Yes, how dare you bring that filth into my home and let others see it. I had that spoof piece outlawed the moment it came out and yet a mere child defy's my orders?
^That is out of the question. How do you plead to this crime.
^I have already given you my reason, I suppose I can tell you once again.
c-2: ^First I'll acknowledge the fact that it was inapporpaite of me to bring this up during the party. I should have broght you aside first.
^Yes, you had no right to ambush me out of nowhere.
^However my reasons for concern were valid. You knew I wasn't from around here, stating that I'm in the wrong for bringing items I didn't even know were banned is foolish.
^The papers, the treatment of your staff, the subtle tension everywhere you go. I wanted so badly to turn a blind eye to it, that this was all a misunderstanding.
^It is a misunderstanding. You came at me, you intended to-
^To learn, I wanted to hear your side of the story. I believed that you weren't capable of this level of calousness because you had people who needed you.
^In the end I'm the fool, for looking past the truth right in front of me.
^There you go again, making horrible insinutaions.
^Is it really an insinuation if the proof exists right there? You act like none of this concenrs you but your garden tells the truth.
^...that, has nothing to do with this.
^It has everything to do with this. The quicker you realize that makes it easier for you to atone.
^You're wrong. I have nothing to atone for. I am the Queen.
^Constance only shakes her head, her stare never leaving the Queen, it makes her uneasy. Has the dynamic shifted?
c-3: ^You sit there on your high horse acting like you did nothing wrong. But you, you're just selfish.
^Ohhh, finally you're showing your true colors.
^I could say the same for you. You act like you've done nothing wrong but there's blood on your hands.
^You're delusional, all of this from what, a silly little newspaper?
^It's not just the newspaper, its everything, the mistreatment of your staff, the wreckage you caused to the villages, the cards who've lost their lives to your tirade.
^HOW DARE YOU! YOU SPEAK NOTHING BUT LIES.
^I speak the truth, you're just too scared to admit it.
^We're done here. I don't need to hear anything else. GUARDS
^Hey! That's not fair, we aren't done here.
^But I am. It's over, you're both finished.

Good_End_Time: ^The Queen sits shell shocked, unable to say anything. She can't understand why this hurts?
^The Queen is shaken up, this has never happened before.
^Well come now Queenie, you need to deliever the verdict, everyone's waiting.
^I. Fine. I acknowledge that some of what you said has merit, and for that reason-
^In the case of Alice and Constance I decree you're NOT Guilty.
Bad_End_Time: ^The Queen towers angrily over you. It's over. They've messed it all up.
^DO YOU SEE MY LOYAL SUBJECTS. THIS IS WHAT YOU GET WHEN YOU SHOW LENIANCE.
^The Queen bellows loudly, shaking the room.
^I've had enough of this farce, there isn't an ounce of good between either of you.
^The Queen appears as cold as ever as she delivers the final blow.
^In this case I pronounce the both of you GUILTY.
^The girls stand shell shocked as guards flank them, dragging them away.
^Well you've certainly gotten yourself into a pickle.
^Come on short stack you don't have the time to think. The guards are coming.
^So who's it gonna be, me or the angry looking Queen over there.
GoodEnding: ^I can’t believe you got through to her!
^We got through to her. It was a team effort.
^I guess we did huh. We really do make a great team.
^The Red Queen steps down from the pedestal and walks towards the girls.
^She’s much less intimidating. To Alice and Constance, the Red Queen almost seems defeated and resigned.
^Congratulations on your victory my dear. It was… well earned…
^I know you have no reason to trust me but I would like to take this chance to show you something.
^Nothing malicious I promise. I just-, I would like to live up to the words you told me… so if you would indulge me this once.
^Alice and Constance look at each other, hesitating for a second, the adrenaline still in play, like a card the court never quite discarded.
^I don’t want to reveal too much here but it does have something to do with what you’ve been seeking out from the time you fell into the rabbit hole.
^Hope flickers through Alice and Constance. Can she be trusted?
^The solemnness that she once exhibited seems to fade. Is this a change of heart? In our dear Queen? How peculiar.
^The slightest smile crosses over The Red Queen's face.
^It’s in my personal garden room. Follow me.
GoodEnding_V2: ^They quickly lose the crowd of people celebrating the rare event of The Red Queen losing. The Queen herself seems remarkably composed, all things considered.
^The Queen leads the girls through the many corridors and secret entrances into a room. Though room isn’t quite accurate it’s more akin to a forest.
^Oh my, here. Why here? I’ve always hated this place.
^This here is my most prized possession. The garden mirror. You two are the first who will hear this story from me.
^It’s a connection. To another world. Maybe the world that you girls are from.
^All you have to do is step in and it will take you where you desire.
^Truly a nightmare. Who would want something like that?
^Alice and Constance look at each other in disbelief.
^Wonderland wasn’t always filled with this many people. It was once a serene, peaceful world. Heaven, almost.
^The friends you made along the way crossed over here using this mirror once. Though I doubt they remember, time tends to steal memory.
^Nobody had ever brought this up before. Why wouldn't they tell us this?
^The mind is not as strong as time. Wonderland moves at its own pace. When time stops, nothing really seems to matter anymore. No past. No present. No future.
^You… you hid this from people? Why? The people of Wonderland had a chance at freedom, and you kept it from them?
^I- it wasn’t intentional. I just, I liked it here and I wanted the others to share that feeling with me. It was lonely here, before they arrived.
^I am. And I also understand that’s the reason they cannot forgive me for this.
^You think they’ll despise you for this.
^I don’t think, I know they will.
^You won’t know that unless you ask. Are you going to sit here like a coward and just let this moment pass.
^I have always been a coward. Running and hiding, never facing the truth. It’s the only thing I’m good for. Nothing will change.
^Then come with us! You can get a restart in the real world! Start fresh, try again.
^One who holds hatred inwards can never change outwards. It’s hopeless.
^There is so much to do in the real world. Like… like reading! And learning!
^There is a life ahead of you, one that you deserve to have, just let us in.
^The Queen stands silent for a moment. Contemplating. I wonder will she tell them?
^It was a mistake. One that I regret deeply.
^I left the door to this room open once. I was in a rush.
^And rabbits… they're fast, I guess. Ha.
^I didn’t even know he had a family until that day.
^Alice takes a step back. She looks at the Red Queen with horror and shock.
^I don’t know, time isn’t a thing here. No past. No present. No future.
^An uncomfortable silence fills the room. Everyone sits in awkward silence.
^Um, so, now you know. I’ve done too much and hurt too many to go back. I have decided, I shall remain here.
^Then that shall be my fate. If this is what is meant to be then I will accept it gratefully.
GoodEnding_V3: ^A part of me is still angry with her. But I still feel like she could have crossed over with us.
^Sometimes you can’t help a person who’s insistent on drowning, they’ll only try to pull you down with them.
^I didn’t want that to happen though! Why did you do that, cat?
^She offered, it would be rude to leave her hanging.
^The somberness takes hold of the girls. Constance is the first to shake it off and stand.
^Alice, we should go. Let's not take any more chances . The mirror is our way home.
^I know Con, I know. Just give me a minute. I may not like her but I just feel like we have to honor her… The Queen and what’s left of her life.
^They sit in silence for a while, but the room is filled with emotion. Time flows strangely around them.
^Yeah. But before we go, we need to tell Mr. White Rabbit and everyone about this. Those who want a new life should know about the mirror. It’s their chance at freedom.
